title: Measurement, SI Units, Dimensional Analysis, and Uncertainty · Worked Case: Area Volume Units
summary: Measure a room, select materials, convert specifications, and report tolerances for a community repair. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg area volume units increment in a unit-safe materials estimate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Square or cube conversion factors when converting area and volume.
Convert quantities with unit factors that equal one and cancel dimensions explicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Area Volume Units evidence
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 7 m by 5 m. Calculate its area.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 7 m × 5 m.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 7 m × 5 m.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes m² or m^2 or square meters.
